name: Acorn Squash with Cranberry Stuffing
recipeInstructions:
1. Halve the acorn squash and remove the seeds.
2. Spray lightly with cooking spray and place face down on a baking sheet.
3. Bake for about 45 to 60 minutes in a 350°F oven or until tender when tested with a fork.
4. Coat a non-stick skillet with cooking spray.
5. Over medium heat, sauté the onion and celery until tender.
6. Add salt, pepper, allspice, apple, and 1 tablespoon of apple juice; cook just until the apple is tender.
7. Stir in cranberries, raisins, and 1 tablespoon of apple juice; cook until the cranberries pop.
8. Add brown sugar; stir until dissolved.
9. Stir in bread cubes and nuts. If desired, a few drops of almond extract may be added.
10. Sprinkle baked squash with salt (optional) and pepper.
11. Divide the stuffing evenly between the halves of the squash.
12. Bake for 15 minutes at 375°F.
Makes 4 side-dish servings.
Per serving: 106 Kcal, 1.1g fat (0.2g sat fat), 9% CFF, 249mg Na
Makes 2 main-course servings.
Per serving: 212 Kcal, 2.2g fat (0.4g sat fat), 9% CFF, 498mg Na
